name: Earlham Savings Bank
description_short: Community bank with 9 branches across Central Iowa offering checking, savings, loans, and agricultural banking since 1931, FDIC-insured.
description_long:
Earlham Savings Bank was established on January 1st, 1931, with the founding principle of being "large enough to serve you, small enough to know you." The bank operates as a traditional community financial institution rooted in Central Iowa, maintaining a strong commitment to customer-first service and fair treatment. Over nearly a century, Earlham has grown from a single location to nine branches serving communities including Bevington, De Soto, Des Moines (two locations), Earlham, Granger, Van Meter, West Des Moines, and Woodward.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of consumer and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, online banking, e-statements, personal loans, business loans, and specialized agricultural banking services. Customers can access accounts through multiple channels: physical branch visits, online banking platforms, ATM networks, and after-hours banking options. The bank also provides loan officer consultations and an online account application process for both deposit accounts and consumer loans.

Earlham Savings Bank distinguishes itself through its community-focused positioning and agricultural banking heritage. The institution emphasizes its deep roots in Central Iowa and commitment to personalized service at a local level. All deposits are FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank actively engages in community programs such as customer shred days for document destruction and provides fraud prevention education to customers.

As a regional community bank, Earlham serves customers who value local banking relationships and personalized service over digital-first platforms. The nine-branch footprint provides reasonable accessibility within Central Iowa, though customers outside this geographic region would need to bank remotely. The bank maintains standard community bank operations without offering the extensive product variety or technology innovation of larger national institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Fraud prevention resources and scam alerts
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Nine branch locations across Central Iowa for in-person service
Specialized agricultural banking services for farm customers
Multiple account access channels including online banking, ATMs, and after-hours options
Free community services like customer shred days for document destruction
Proactive fraud prevention education and scam alerts
Fast and secure online account application process
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Central Iowa with no out-of-state branches
No mention of competitive interest rates, promotional offers, or rate comparisons on website
Limited transparency on fee structures, minimum balance requirements, or account features
No mention of mobile app or digital-first banking capabilities beyond basic online banking
No information about business banking product details, credit card offerings, or investment services

contact.address: 7300 Lake Dr, West Des Moines, IA 50266

best_for: Central Iowa residents seeking personalized community banking with local branch access
Agricultural business owners needing farm-specific lending and banking services
Customers who prefer face-to-face relationships with bank staff over digital-only banking
Depositors prioritizing FDIC insurance and traditional bank stability over fintech features
diagnosis: Earlham Savings Bank is best for Central Iowa residents and agricultural business owners seeking community-focused banking with local branch relationships and FDIC insurance. The main caveat is geographic limitation—the bank operates only nine branches in Central Iowa with no national presence, making it unsuitable for customers outside this region or those requiring extensive digital banking capabilities.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
